# Technical Specification — #27 Replace qode ticket fetch with MCP server integration

*Spec generated by qode. Copy to GitHub issue #27 for team review.*

---

## 1. Feature Overview

Replace the five hand-rolled HTTP ticket provider clients in `internal/ticket/` and the `qode ticket fetch` CLI command with an IDE-native MCP approach. The `/qode-ticket-fetch` slash command is rewritten to instruct the AI to use whatever MCP servers are configured in the IDE to fetch the ticket — including following linked resources (Figma designs, Google Docs, SharePoint documents, Confluence pages, Miro boards) via their own MCP servers if available.

**Business value:** Eliminates maintenance of five diverging API clients (~500 LOC deleted); unlocks rich ticket context (comments, attachments, linked designs) that the old flat-file approach silently dropped; aligns with the MCP ecosystem already present in Cursor and Claude Code; reduces the attack surface for credential handling.

**Success criteria:**
- `go build ./...` passes after all deletions
- `go test ./...` passes with updated scaffold tests
- `/qode-ticket-fetch` in both IDEs emits an MCP-driven prompt that collects full ticket content and follows linked resources
- `docs/how-to-use-ticket-fetch.md` provides actionable MCP setup instructions for all five ticketing systems and five linked-resource services

---

## 2. Scope

### In scope
- Delete `internal/ticket/` (8 files) and `internal/cli/ticket.go`
- Remove `newTicketCmd()` registration from `internal/cli/root.go`
- Remove `TicketSystem TicketSystemConfig` from `internal/config/schema.go`; delete `TicketSystemConfig` and `AuthConfig` types (confirmed: no other consumers)
- Rewrite `qode-ticket-fetch` slash command content in `internal/scaffold/claudecode.go` and `internal/scaffold/cursor.go` — always MCP, always follows linked resources
- Update `internal/scaffold/scaffold_test.go` — 4 existing tests plus 1 new test
- Rewrite `docs/how-to-use-ticket-fetch.md` with MCP setup for 5 ticketing systems + 5 linked-resource services
- Update `docs/qode-yaml-reference.md` (remove `ticket_system` section)
- Update `README.md` (ticket system section)
- Regenerate `.claude/commands/qode-ticket-fetch.md` and `.cursor/commands/qode-ticket-fetch.mdc` via `qode init`

### Out of scope
- Building a Go MCP client
- draw.io MCP support (no official server exists)
- Changes to `internal/context/context.go` or prompt templates
- Backward compatibility for `ticket_system:` in `qode.yaml`
- Making the context file structure configurable

### Assumptions
- Users have Cursor or Claude Code with MCP support configured
- Go's `yaml.v3` silently ignores unknown fields — existing `qode.yaml` files with `ticket_system:` will not error after schema removal
- `AuthConfig` is only consumed by `TicketSystemConfig` (confirmed by grep)

---

## 3. Architecture & Design

### Current architecture

```
qode ticket fetch <url>
        │
        ▼
internal/cli/ticket.go
        │  cfg.TicketSystem (Type, URL, ProjectKey, Auth)
        ▼
internal/ticket/ticket.go  DetectProvider(url, cfg)
        │
        ├── jira.go
        ├── azuredevops.go
        ├── linear.go
        ├── github.go
        └── notion.go
                │
                ▼
        context/ticket.md  (title + body only)
```

### New architecture

```
/qode-ticket-fetch <url>   (IDE slash command)
        │
        ▼
AI reads MCP prompt from:
  .claude/commands/qode-ticket-fetch.md   (Claude Code)
  .cursor/commands/qode-ticket-fetch.mdc  (Cursor)
        │
        ▼
AI calls IDE-managed MCP servers:
  ┌─────────────────────┐   ┌──────────────────────┐
  │  Ticket system MCP  │   │  Linked resource MCP  │
  │  (Jira / Linear /   │   │  (Figma / GDrive /    │
  │   GitHub / Notion / │   │   SharePoint /        │
  │   Azure DevOps)     │   │   Confluence / Miro)  │
  └─────────────────────┘   └──────────────────────┘
        │
        ▼
AI writes to context/:
  context/ticket.md          (title, description, metadata)
  context/ticket-comments.md (all comments with author + timestamp)
  context/ticket-links.md    (linked resources with summaries)
```

### Affected layers

| Layer | Change type |
|---|---|
| `internal/ticket/` | **Deleted** |
| `internal/cli/ticket.go` | **Deleted** |
| `internal/cli/root.go` | **Modified** — remove 1 line + stale import |
| `internal/config/schema.go` | **Modified** — remove 2 types + 1 field |
| `internal/scaffold/claudecode.go` | **Modified** — new `ticketFetchClaudeCommand` helper |
| `internal/scaffold/cursor.go` | **Modified** — new `ticketFetchCursorCommand` helper |
| `internal/scaffold/scaffold_test.go` | **Modified** — 4 updated + 1 new test |
| `docs/how-to-use-ticket-fetch.md` | **Rewritten** |
| `docs/qode-yaml-reference.md` | **Modified** — remove section |
| `README.md` | **Modified** — update ticket section |
| `.claude/commands/qode-ticket-fetch.md` | **Regenerated** |
| `.cursor/commands/qode-ticket-fetch.mdc` | **Regenerated** |

---

## 4. Interface Contracts

### Claude Code slash command — `internal/scaffold/claudecode.go`

**Function:** `ticketFetchClaudeCommand(name string) string`

**Returns:**
```markdown
# Fetch Ticket via MCP — <name>

Fetch the ticket at the URL or ID provided in $ARGUMENTS using your available MCP tools.

**Steps:**
1. Use the MCP tool for the appropriate ticketing system (Jira, Linear, GitHub Issues,
   Notion, or Azure DevOps). Prefer the official MCP server for the system. If no MCP
   server is available, open the URL in a browser tool and extract the content.
2. Collect: title, description, all comments (with author names and timestamps), any
   linked resources, and attachment summaries.
3. For each linked resource, fetch its content using the appropriate MCP server if one
   is configured — e.g. Figma (designs), Google Docs/Drive, SharePoint, Confluence,
   Miro boards. If no MCP server is available for a linked resource, record its URL
   and title only.
4. Write the following files under context/, creating the directory if needed:
   - context/ticket.md — title, description, metadata
   - context/ticket-comments.md — all comments in chronological order (omit if no comments)
   - context/ticket-links.md — linked resources with summaries or fetched content (omit if no links)
5. Report a one-line summary: "Fetched: <title> — <N> comments, <M> links."
```

**Invariants:**
- Must NOT start with `!` (would be treated as a shell command by Claude Code)
- Must contain `$ARGUMENTS`
- Must name all three output file paths

### Cursor slash command — `internal/scaffold/cursor.go`

**Function:** `ticketFetchCursorCommand(name string) string`

**Returns:** same steps as above, prepended with YAML frontmatter:
```markdown
---
description: Fetch a ticket into branch context for <name>
---

Fetch the ticket at the URL or ID provided in $ARGUMENTS using your available MCP tools.

[same steps 1–5]
```

### `claudeSlashCommands(name string)` map entry change

**Before:** `"qode-ticket-fetch": \`!qode ticket fetch $ARGUMENTS\``
**After:** `"qode-ticket-fetch": ticketFetchClaudeCommand(name)`

### `slashCommands(name string)` map entry change

**Before:** inline `fmt.Sprintf(...)` with `qode ticket fetch $ARGUMENTS`
**After:** `"qode-ticket-fetch": ticketFetchCursorCommand(name)`

---

## 5. Data Model Changes

### `internal/config/schema.go`

**Remove from `Config` struct:**
```go
TicketSystem TicketSystemConfig `yaml:"ticket_system,omitempty"`
```

**Delete types entirely:**
```go
type TicketSystemConfig struct { ... }  // and all fields
type AuthConfig struct { ... }          // confirmed no other consumers
```

**Migration strategy:** None required. Go's `yaml.v3` silently ignores unknown fields — existing `qode.yaml` files with `ticket_system:` will parse cleanly. Users should remove the stale block manually; the updated `docs/qode-yaml-reference.md` will note its removal.

### Context file structure (written by AI, not by Go code)

No Go struct changes. The AI writes these files directly:

| File | Content |
|---|---|
| `context/ticket.md` | Title, description, metadata (always written) |
| `context/ticket-comments.md` | Comments with author + timestamp (omitted if none) |
| `context/ticket-links.md` | Linked resources with summaries (omitted if none) |

`internal/context/context.go` auto-loads all files in `context/` via `Extra []string` — no changes needed.

---

## 6. Implementation Tasks

- [ ] **Task 1 (delete):** Remove `internal/ticket/` directory — delete `ticket.go`, `jira.go`, `azuredevops.go`, `linear.go`, `github.go`, `notion.go`, `github_test.go`, `notion_test.go`
- [ ] **Task 2 (delete):** Delete `internal/cli/ticket.go`; remove `newTicketCmd()` at `internal/cli/root.go:68`; remove now-stale `ticket` import from `root.go` if present; run `go build ./...` to confirm clean
- [ ] **Task 3 (schema):** `internal/config/schema.go` — remove `TicketSystem TicketSystemConfig` from `Config`; delete `TicketSystemConfig` and `AuthConfig` types; run `go build ./...`
- [ ] **Task 4 (scaffold/claude):** `internal/scaffold/claudecode.go` — add `ticketFetchClaudeCommand(name string) string` helper; replace inline `"qode-ticket-fetch"` value with `ticketFetchClaudeCommand(name)`
- [ ] **Task 5 (scaffold/cursor):** `internal/scaffold/cursor.go` — add `ticketFetchCursorCommand(name string) string` helper; replace inline `"qode-ticket-fetch"` value with `ticketFetchCursorCommand(name)`
- [ ] **Task 6 (tests):** `internal/scaffold/scaffold_test.go` — update `TestClaudeSlashCommands_ContainsTicketFetch`, `TestSetupClaudeCode_WritesTicketFetchCommand`, `TestCursorSlashCommands_ContainsTicketFetch`, `TestSetupCursor_WritesTicketFetchCommand` to assert MCP content; add `TestClaudeSlashCommands_TicketFetchNoExclamationPrefix`
- [ ] **Task 7 (regen):** Run `qode init` to regenerate `.claude/commands/qode-ticket-fetch.md` and `.cursor/commands/qode-ticket-fetch.mdc`; commit regenerated files
- [ ] **Task 8 (docs):** Rewrite `docs/how-to-use-ticket-fetch.md`; update `docs/qode-yaml-reference.md`; update `README.md`

**Order:** 1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5 → 6 → 7 → 8. Tasks 1/2/3 are deletion tasks ordered for clean build verification at each step. Tasks 4/5 are independent of each other. Tasks 6/7 depend on 4+5. Task 8 can be done in parallel with 4–7.

## 7. Testing Strategy

### Unit tests — `internal/scaffold/scaffold_test.go`

**Tests to update** (currently assert old `!qode ticket fetch $ARGUMENTS` content):

| Test | New assertion |
|---|---|
| `TestClaudeSlashCommands_ContainsTicketFetch` | `strings.Contains(content, "context/ticket.md")` and `strings.Contains(content, "$ARGUMENTS")` |
| `TestSetupClaudeCode_WritesTicketFetchCommand` | Same content assertions on file read |
| `TestCursorSlashCommands_ContainsTicketFetch` | `strings.Contains(content, "context/ticket.md")` and `strings.Contains(content, "description:")` |
| `TestSetupCursor_WritesTicketFetchCommand` | Same content assertions on file read |

**New test:**

```go
func TestClaudeSlashCommands_TicketFetchNoExclamationPrefix(t *testing.T) {
    cmds := claudeSlashCommands("testproject")
    content := cmds["qode-ticket-fetch"]
    if strings.HasPrefix(content, "!") {
        t.Errorf("qode-ticket-fetch must not start with '!', got: %q", content[:min(len(content), 40)])
    }
}
```

**Additional assertions** to add to the updated ticket-fetch tests:
- Content contains `"Figma"` or `"linked resource"` (linked-resource instructions present)
- Content does NOT contain `"qode ticket fetch"` (no CLI reference)

### Integration tests — `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`
- After Task 2: `go build ./...` must pass — confirms no orphaned imports
- After Task 3: `go build ./...` must pass — confirms no `TicketSystem` consumers remain
- Full `go test ./...` run after Task 6

### E2E / manual verification
- Run `qode init` (Task 7) on this repo; inspect `.claude/commands/qode-ticket-fetch.md` — must contain MCP instructions, must not start with `!`
- Run `qode init`; inspect `.cursor/commands/qode-ticket-fetch.mdc` — must contain `description:` frontmatter and MCP instructions

### Edge cases to test explicitly
- `claudeSlashCommands("testproject")["qode-ticket-fetch"]` does not start with `!`
- Cursor command contains `description:` YAML frontmatter
- Both commands contain `$ARGUMENTS`
- Both commands reference `context/ticket.md`
- Neither command references `qode ticket fetch` as a shell invocation

---

## 8. Security Considerations

### Credential handling — improved
The old providers read `JIRA_API_TOKEN`, `AZURE_DEVOPS_PAT`, `LINEAR_API_KEY`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `NOTION_API_KEY` from environment variables loaded via `.env`. Deleting the providers removes all credential handling from the Go binary. Credentials are now managed entirely by the IDE's MCP server configuration — outside qode's attack surface.

### Prompt injection via project name
`ticketFetchClaudeCommand(name string)` and `ticketFetchCursorCommand(name string)` interpolate `name` (= `filepath.Base(root)`) into the prompt via `fmt.Sprintf`. Directory names cannot contain newlines on Linux/macOS/Windows, eliminating the newline-based prompt injection vector. No additional sanitisation is required.

### Input validation
`$ARGUMENTS` is passed directly to the AI as the ticket URL — no Go-side URL parsing or validation. The MCP server validates the URL when it processes the request. This is the correct boundary.

### No new authentication or authorisation surfaces introduced.

---

## 9. Open Questions

None — all ambiguities were resolved during requirements refinement:
- Always MCP, no mode field, alpha stage
- `qode ticket fetch` deleted (not deprecated)
- `qode init` is the regeneration command (not `qode ide sync`)
- draw.io explicitly out of scope (no official MCP server)
- `AuthConfig` confirmed safe to delete (no other consumers)
